background: The following data were drawn one observation at a time in the order records: ggbggbgggbgbggbbbggg. Here, $b$ denotes a defective item and $g$ denotes a good item. The experiment was performed to test the hypothesis: $$\mathrm{H}_0: \mathrm{p}=\mathrm{p}_0=0.10 \text { versus } \mathrm{H}_1: \mathrm{p}=\mathrm{p}_1=0.20 .$$ where $p$ denotes the proportion of defective items in the population. It is desired to reject $\mathrm{H}_0$ when it is true with probability 0.05 and to accept $\mathrm{H}_0$ when $\mathrm{H}_1$ is true with probability 0.20. Using a sequential testing plan, determine the decision based on the observations provided.

task.query: Using the sequential testing plan, determine whether you would accept or reject the lot based on the observations: ggbggbgggbgbggbbbggg.
task.answer: The sampling is stopped at the $16^{\text {th }}$ observation and that the lot would be rejected.
